deviations.description: The process model enforces that anesthesia is applied immediately after patient preparation, as shown by the direct sequence from 'Prepare Patient' to 'Administer Anaesthesia' in the process diagram on page 1. Version 2 relaxes this requirement, allowing anesthesia to be applied at any later point after preparation. The process unnecessarily restricts behavior by not permitting any delay between preparation and anesthesia.

The compliance pattern in the process model matches the 'immediate_successor' constraint from Version 1, but Version 2 only requires 'eventually_follows'. The process continues to enforce the stricter, outdated constraint, not reflecting the relaxed timing allowed in Version 2.
process_model_reference: Control flow between Prepare Patient and Administer Anaesthesia

deviations.description: The process model uses the activity names 'prepare_patient' and 'apply_anesthesia', which correspond to Version 1. Version 2 changes these to 'patient_preparation' and 'anesthesia', but the process model does not reflect this update. This results in the process enforcing constraints on the old activity names, which may not fully align with the intent or scope of the new requirement.
